fields.signals: Array<string> — see DomainAdvisory interface in lib/domain-advisory.ts

backward_compatible.rationale: New field only. No existing field was removed, renamed, or changed type. Consumers that do not read domain_advisory are unaffected.

downstream_consumers_updated.rationale: No existing consumer was reading domain_advisory before this change (field did not exist). Consumers that process governance_report.json nodeSignals now receive MODERATE-risk entries they previously did not see — this is additive, not breaking.

verdict_or_semantic_changed.value: no — automated decisions unchanged; reviewer routing surface changed

detail.reviewer_visibility_detail: governance_report.json nodeSignals now contains 4 MODERATE-risk docs (lenovo-dmc, lenovo-one-key-update-tool-, linux-usb-hid-description, -hp-firmware-installer-macos-*) that were not surfaced before. Reviewers and AI governance framework consumers reading nodeSignals see new routing information.

files_referencing_domain_advisory: lib/domain-advisory.ts — defines DomainAdvisory interface and computeDomainAdvisory()
scripts/advisory-replay.ts — writes domain_advisory back to audit.json in-place
scripts/production-wave-runner.ts — computes and stores domain_advisory; exposes risk_level/signals/corpus_overlap_score in govNodes which feeds governance_report.json
lib_consumer_analysis.governance_report_nodeSignals_routing: advisory_risk_level !== 'NONE' → included in nodeSignals array in governance_report.json (reviewer-visible, not a blocking gate)

classification_verdict.risk_assessment: LOW — additive field, verdict-neutral, no existing gate logic modified
